input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | clang, git, etc. |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android toolchain |

---

## 2. Installation Steps

```bash
# 1. Install .NET SDK (use the macOS installer from dot.net)
#    Verify:
dotnet --version

# 2. Install the MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# 3. Accept Xcode license (after installing Xcode from App Store)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 4. Install Xcode simulators (via Xcode → Settings → Platforms)

# 5. Android SDK/NDK/JDK are provisioned automatically by the
#    maui-android workload. To customize paths, set env vars:
#    ANDROID_HOME, JAVA_HOME
```

---

## 3. Discovering the Right Versions

- **Which .NET SDK?** → `dotnet --list-sdks` and check [dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy) for supported versions.
- **Which MAUI version?** → Tied to the SDK. Run `dotnet workload list` to confirm installed version.
- **Which Xcode?** → Check [Microsoft's MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) — each MAUI release states the minimum Xcode version.
- **Which Android API level?** → The workload installs a compatible SDK. Override via `$(TargetFramework)` in your `.csproj` (e.g., `net8.0-android35`).

---

## 4. Verification

```bash
# Confirm workloads
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, maui-ios, maui-android, etc.

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

---

## 5. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight editor with Hot Reload support.
- **Android Emulator** — create via `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager` or through VS Code.
- **`dotnet maui check`** (from [maui-check](https://github.com/redth/dotnet-maui-check)) — community tool that audits your environment and fixes gaps.

---

## Key Official Links

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| Install guide | https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation |
| Release notes | https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/ |
| .NET downloads | https://dot.net/download |
| Xcode releases | https://developer.apple.com/xcode/ |

Follow the install guide link above — it's the single canonical source maintained by the MAUI team and always reflects current version requirements.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they just need the information/plan. This is a knowledge question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a comprehensive answer directly.
